# Practice Direction 36ZG – Part 3 of the Domestic Abuse Act 2021: Provision during piloted commencement

This Practice Direction supplements rule 36.2A FPR (Domestic Abuse Act 2021: provision during pilot commencement of Part 3).


## I — SCOPE OF THIS PRACTICE DIRECTION AND INTERPRETATION


## 1. Scope and application of the Family Procedure Rules 2010 and associated practice directions


1.1       This practice direction applies to proceedings in the family court and Family Division of the High Court under Part 3 of the Domestic Abuse Act 2021 during the piloted commencement of that Part.


1.2       The Family Procedure Rules 2010 (FPR) and associated practice directions apply to proceedings to which this practice direction applies, but where a provision of the FPR or practice directions conflict with this practice direction, this practice direction will take precedence.


1.3       During the operation of the pilot—


(a)        rule 16.6 FPR will apply as modified by paragraph 1.4; and


(b)        Practice Direction 5A will apply as modified by paragraph 1.5.


1.4       In rule 16.6 FPR, after paragraph (1)(d) insert—


“(e)       under Part 3 of the Domestic Abuse Act 2021”.


1.5       In Practice Direction 5A—


(a)        in Table 1, after the row relating to Part 34 Reciprocal Enforcement of Maintenance Orders insert—


(b)        in Table 2, after the row for form D651 insert—
